Full-stack data map

DATA FLOW — lines = data moving: store reads/writes, server push/pull, DOM, + teal call-hops that carry data toward a store/server.

legend & how to read this

Legend

Vertical position = what the code actually does (read from its data edges, not its folder). Bottom→top: the page (reader.blade.php) ▸ code that bridges page↔IndexedDB ▸ the IndexedDB object stores ▸ code that bridges IndexedDB↔server ▸ PostgreSQL tables.

Horizontal column = source folder (distinguished by colour): indexedDB, hyperlights, hypercites, divEditor. So each box's column is its folder and its row is what it does. A box sitting in a row that doesn't match its folder's natural role (e.g. a hyperlights file up in the IndexedDB-store row) = code acting out of role — a candidate to move when restructuring.

Detail level (top-bar dropdown): min shows one box per top-level folder, default one box per module, max every function. Double-click a folder box to drill into its modules, a module box into its functions (and again to collapse). Changing the level keeps your current selection lit.

Single-click to trace — and what it traces depends on the lens. In the data-flow lens it follows the data along the map's vertical axis. The trace: button sets the direction — to DOM ▾ = data heading down to the page (load: from Postgres or IndexedDB → DOM) / to PostgreSQL ▴ = data heading up to the DB (save: from the DOM or IndexedDB → Postgres) / both — and re-applies live. With nothing selected, that button lights the whole pipeline in that direction. A typed Postgres table or IndexedDB store traces its precise TS-type lineage (the functions that actually handle that record), not every connected function; any other node lights its load/save pathway. In the coupling lens it follows the full dependency reach (every module this one transitively touches), and red edges mark a feedback loop — the path returns to where it started (a circular dependency). The rest dims but stays visible. Double-click a module box to drill into its files (and again to collapse). Navigate: scroll to zoom, drag the canvas to pan (nodes don't drag), fit to reset. Click a line or empty space to clear a trace.

The show code coupling button flips the whole map to a second lens: lines become which function calls which (the code's internal wiring); orange = a call crossing folders (modules reaching into each other).

The find circular deps button flips to the IMPORT lens (module→module dependencies) and tells the truth about cycles: red = a real static-import ring (the only kind that risks a TDZ "Cannot access X before initialization" crash — break these); orange dashed = a dynamic-import cycle-breaker — a back-edge that would form a static ring, deferred to runtime with await import() (safe, but structural debt: a bidirectional import that ideally becomes one-way via events/DI); teal dashed = a lazy-load — a dynamic import with no cycle (genuine code-splitting, fine). The lazy-loads button isolates just those teal edges — your JS-loading-optimisation surface (what's deferred into separate chunks).
